originalTitle: South African president says he will not step down after impeachment call
title: Our President Stays! Good News for South Africa!
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hi everyone! I am your friendly African news reporter. I am here to tell you about some important news from a super cool country. This country is called South Africa. It is in the southern part of our big continent, Africa. South Africa is a beautiful place. It has amazing animals like lions and elephants. It has big, tall mountains and sunny beaches too. Many children like you live in South Africa. They go to school and play with their friends. Just like you do!

Meet President Ramaphosa!

Every country has a leader. This leader helps to make sure everything runs smoothly. In South Africa, the leader is a very important person. His name is President Cyril Ramaphosa. Can you say that? Cyril Ramaphosa. He is like the head teacher of the whole country! Imagine having to look after so many people. That is a very big job. He works hard every day. He wants to make South Africa a great place for everyone. He cares about all the people, especially the children. He wants you to have good schools. He wants you to have nice homes. He wants you to be safe and happy. That is why his job is so important. Did you know? A president is chosen by the people of the country. They vote for the person they think will do the best job. It's like choosing your class captain, but for a whole country!

Big News: Our President is Staying!

Now for the big news! For a few days, some people were talking. They were asking if President Ramaphosa would leave his job. This is called "stepping down" or "resigning." It means giving up his important role as president. But guess what? He said "No!" He told everyone that he will not step down. He will stay in his job as the President of South Africa. This happened on Monday night. So, he is still our president. He will continue to lead the country. This made many people happy.

Why Were People Talking?

You might wonder why people were talking about him leaving. Well, sometimes big jobs come with big questions. There were some questions about something that happened a little while ago. These questions were about some money that was on his farm. People wanted to know more about it. So, some people said maybe he should not be president anymore. They asked him to leave. This is what we call an an "impeachment call." It's like when someone asks if a class captain should not be captain anymore. It’s a very serious thing. But President Ramaphosa listened to everyone. He thought about it very carefully. And then he made his decision. He decided to stay.

Why He Wants to Stay

President Ramaphosa said he wants to stay because he loves South Africa. He believes he can still do a very good job for all the people. He wants to help solve problems. He wants to make life better for families. He wants to build more schools and hospitals. He wants to make sure there are good roads. He wants all children to have a bright future. He thinks his work is not finished yet. He feels it is his duty to continue leading the country. He knows his job is to serve the people. He wants to keep working hard for you and for your families. That is why he chose to stay in his big job. Did you know? South Africa is famous for its beautiful flowers, especially the Protea! It's their national flower.

What Happens Next? The Parliament Helps

So, President Ramaphosa is staying. But the questions about what happened on his farm are still there. What happens next? Well, there is a very important place called Parliament. Imagine a very big house where other important people work. These people are called Members of Parliament. They also help the country. They help make important rules and talk about big decisions. They are like a big team that helps the president. Now, these people in Parliament will talk about the questions related to President Ramaphosa. They will look at everything very carefully. They will decide what to do next. It is like a big meeting where everyone tries to find the best way forward. We will watch and see what they decide. They will make sure things are fair. Did you know? Parliament is like a big school assembly hall, but for the whole country! Important people meet there to talk about the country's rules and plans.

What This Means for You!

You might be a young boy or girl in South Africa. Or maybe you are in another part of Africa! You might wonder what all this means for you. Well, the president's job is to keep the country safe and running well. When the president stays, it means he continues to work on things that are important for you. He will keep working to make sure schools are open. He will keep working to make sure there are doctors when you are sick. He will keep working to make sure there is food for everyone. So, you can still go to school. You can still play with your friends. Life goes on. Our leaders are always working to make our countries better for us. They want us to grow up strong and happy.

Our Wonderful Africa

Our continent, Africa, is full of amazing stories and people. We have so many different countries. We have so many different leaders. Everyone tries their best to make things good. It is important to know what is happening in our countries. Even when things are a bit tricky, we always look for the good. We work together to build a brighter future. South Africa is a strong country. It has strong people. And it has a president who says he wants to keep working for them. Let’s wish President Ramaphosa and all the people of South Africa well. Let's hope for a peaceful and happy future for everyone!
summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa of South Africa has said he will not leave his big job, choosing to stay and lead the country after some people asked him to step down.
whyMatters: This is important because the president is like the head teacher for the whole country, and he helps make sure kids have schools, homes, and stay safe, so his decision to stay means he will keep working to make South Africa a good place for everyone.

originalTitle: Dozens of Nigerian fishermen feared dead after Chad air strikes on Boko Haram
title: Dozens of Nigerian fishermen feared dead after Chad air strikes on Boko Haram
content: A fishermen's leader tells the BBC that 40 people are feared dead  - either in the strikes or drowning as they tried to flee.

originalTitle: Whales could be harmed by diverted ships avoiding Middle East, scientists warn
title: Big Ships and Our Whale Friends: A Story from Africa!
content:
Hello, Little Explorers!

Hello, my wonderful friends! I'm so happy to be here, your friendly news reporter from amazing Africa. We live on a continent with so much to see, from tall mountains to wide-open plains, and of course, our beautiful, big blue oceans! Today, we're going to talk about something special happening in our ocean. It's about giant ships and our incredible whale friends. Let's dive in!

What are These Big Ships?

Imagine a toy boat, but super, super, super big! That's kind of like a ship. These ships are like giant floating cities. They are huge! Much bigger than your house, or even your school. These big ships travel across the oceans all over the world. They carry so many things we use every day. Guess what they carry? They carry your toys, your clothes, yummy food like bananas and rice, and even parts for your mum and dad's car! So, these ships are very important because they bring us so many things from far away. Did you know? Some of the biggest ships in the world are longer than three football fields put together! That's super, super long!

Why Are Ships Changing Their Path?

Now, imagine you want to go to your friend's house. Usually, you take one path. But what if that path suddenly became a bit tricky or not safe? Maybe there are big puddles or bumpy rocks. What would you do? You would choose a different, safer path, right? That's exactly what big ships are doing! They are changing their usual way of travelling. There are some tricky situations happening far away, in a place called the Middle East. It's not safe for the ships to go their usual way through that area. So, since a time called 2023, many ships have decided to take a new, longer path. They want to keep everyone and everything on the ship safe. It's always best to be safe when you are travelling, even for giant ships!

Around the Tip of Africa!

Where is this new path? It's right here, around the southern part of our very own continent, Africa! It's a special place called the Cape of Good Hope. Have you ever seen a map of Africa? If you look at the bottom, the very pointy end, that's the Cape of Good Hope! It's a beautiful place where the land meets the huge, blue ocean. The ships are sailing all the way around this tip of Africa to get to where they need to go. It's a long journey, but it's a safer one for them. Did you know? The Cape of Good Hope is famous because it's where two great oceans meet – the Atlantic Ocean and the Indian Ocean! It's like a watery handshake between two giant oceans!

Our Amazing Whale Friends

Now, let's talk about our wonderful ocean neighbours: whales! Whales are not fish; they are like us, mammals! But they live in the ocean. They are the biggest animals in the whole wide world! Imagine an animal bigger than a big bus, or even two big buses! That's a whale! They swim, they jump, and they even sing beautiful songs under the water. We have many different kinds of whales that love the warm waters around Africa. They live and play right here in our oceans. Did you know? Whales breathe air, just like you and me! They have to come up to the top of the water to take a big breath, then they dive back down into the deep blue.

How Can Ships Hurt Whales?

Here's the part that makes us a little bit sad. When ships take this new path around the Cape of Good Hope, they are going through places where our whale friends live and play. Imagine you're playing in your garden, and suddenly a giant car drives through it very fast. That would be scary, right? 1. **Bumps and Bruises:** Sometimes, a very big ship might accidentally bump into a whale. Whales are big, but ships are even bigger and heavier! A bump from a big ship can really hurt a whale, making them sick or even stopping them from swimming properly. This is very dangerous for them. 2. **Loud Noises:** Ships also make a lot of noise under the water. Imagine trying to sleep or talk when there's a very loud construction site nearby. It's hard to hear anything else, right? Whales use sounds to talk to each other, find food, and even find their babies. All the ship noise can make it hard for them to do these important things. It's like trying to find your friend in a very noisy marketplace! They can get lost or confused.

Why Is It a Bigger Problem Now?

Before, only some ships took this path around Africa. But now, because of the tricky situations far away, many, many more ships are sailing around the Cape of Good Hope. It's like if only a few cars used a road, but now thousands of cars use it every day. More ships mean more chances for them to meet a whale. More ships mean more noise in the ocean. This makes scientists, who are clever people who study animals and the world, a bit worried. They see a bigger risk for our whale friends.

What Do Scientists Say?

Scientists are like detectives for nature. They watch the whales, they listen to the ocean, and they try to understand what's happening. They have seen that more ships are coming this way, and they are worried about our whale friends. They are saying, "Hey! We need to be careful! Our whales need our help!" They are telling everyone that we need to find ways to make sure the ships can still travel safely, but also that our amazing whales can live safely in their ocean homes. It's a tricky puzzle, but scientists are very good at puzzles! They want to find smart solutions for both the ships and the whales.

What Can We Do to Help?

So, what can be done to help our whale friends? There are some good ideas! * **Slow Down:** If ships go a little bit slower in places where whales like to be, it gives the whales more time to swim away from the big ships. It also gives the ship captains more time to see the whales and steer away. Like driving slowly near a school! * **Look Out:** The people driving the ships, called captains, can be extra careful and look out for whales. They can have special people watching the water to spot whales before they get too close. * **New Paths:** Maybe scientists can help find new, safe paths for ships that are not where many whales live. Like finding a different road for big trucks so they don't drive through residential areas. * **Quiet Please:** We can try to make ships quieter under the water so whales can hear each other and live peacefully. Less noise means whales can talk and play without loud interruptions.

Let's Protect Our Ocean Friends!

It's wonderful that our continent, Africa, is such an important place for ships and for whales. We want to make sure everyone can be safe. Our ocean is a home for so many incredible animals, and whales are some of the most special. By learning about this, even you, my little explorer, are helping! You can tell your friends and family about our amazing whales and why it's important to keep them safe. We can all be guardians of our beautiful blue ocean and its wonderful creatures. Let's keep learning and caring for our world!
summary: Big ships are taking a new, longer path around the tip of Africa to stay safe, but scientists are worried this new route might accidentally hurt our amazing whale friends who live in those oceans.
whyMatters: It's important because whales are special ocean animals that need a safe home, and we want to help big ships travel without accidentally bumping into or disturbing our wonderful whale friends.

title: Hundreds displaced, medical services suspended amid gang violence in Haiti

originalTitle: Calvert-Lewin penalty earns Leeds draw at Tottenham
title: Calvert-Lewin penalty earns Leeds draw at Tottenham
content: Tottenham Hotspur squander the opportunity to take a significant step towards Premier League safety as they are held to a 1-1 draw at home by Leeds United.
